prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ARIMA', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.4371) and high seasonality variance (0.9761), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA leads in trend tracking while THETA dominates overall RMSE and early horizon performance. The presence of both high disagreement and high seasonal variance strongly supports DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths across horizons and pattern types.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ARIMA",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "FT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "FT_rf", "ARIMA", "FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "FT_svr", "CWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["rf", "CWT_rf", "catboost", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "NaiveMovingAverage", "svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.4371,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.4371) and high seasonality variance (0.9761), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA leads in trend tracking while THETA dominates overall RMSE and early horizon performance. The presence of both high disagreement and high seasonal variance strongly supports DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths across horizons and pattern types."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": true,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong per-horizon model switching, favoring selection methods. rmse_spread_ratio=1.4371 and high_model_disagreement=true justify dba_combination (explicitly recommended by pattern_analyst). THETA RMSE=1084 vs CWT_svr RMSE=3065 shows extreme dispersion requiring shrinkage in inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35. horizon_heterogeneous=true supports diverse candidate mix. force_debate=true because top candidates are within 2% score."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - all use rolling/expanding window approach with train_window=5 from eval_config. Diversity is sufficient (≥2 different types present). Universe leaderboards show trimmed_mean candidates perform well on RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE but current set's best (topk_mean_per_horizon_k5, score 0.859) already outperforms them in overall scoring. No redundancy or homogeneous issues detected.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["trimmed_mean_r0.15", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k15"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current best RMSE is 1144.47 but universe has trimmed_mean_r0.15 at 1110.35 and topk_mean_per_horizon_k15 at 1110.40. n_windows=3 (low) suggests stronger regularization - k15 provides better variance reduction for few windows. relative_spread_mean=0.11 < 0.25 so disagreement is moderate, no need for DBA/trimmed_mean with high trim. Adding these two improves RMSE while maintaining diversity.", "changes": ["added trimmed_mean_r0.15 for low n_windows regularization", "added topk_mean_per_horizon_k15 for better variance reduction with few windows"], "when_good": "When relative_spread_mean < 0.25 and n_unique_winners >= 3, focus on adding selection candidates from universe leaderboards that beat current best RMSE."}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.4371) and high seasonality variance (0.9761), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA leads in trend tracking while THETA dominates overall RMSE and early horizon performance. The presence of both high disagreement and high seasonal variance strongly supports DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths across horizons and pattern types.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong per-horizon model switching, favoring selection methods. rmse_spread_ratio=1.4371 and high_model_disagreement=true justify dba_combination (explicitly recommended by pattern_analyst). THETA RMSE=1084 vs CWT_svr RMSE=3065 shows extreme dispersion requiring shrinkage in inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35. horizon_heterogeneous=true supports diverse candidate mix. force_debate=true because top candidates are within 2% score.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - all use rolling/expanding window approach with train_window=5 from eval_config. Diversity is sufficient (≥2 different types present). Universe leaderboards show trimmed_mean candidates perform well on RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE but current set's best (topk_mean_per_horizon_k5, score 0.859) already outperforms them in overall scoring. No redundancy or homogeneous issues detected.

statistician.rationale: Current best RMSE is 1144.47 but universe has trimmed_mean_r0.15 at 1110.35 and topk_mean_per_horizon_k15 at 1110.40. n_windows=3 (low) suggests stronger regularization - k15 provides better variance reduction for few windows. relative_spread_mean=0.11 < 0.25 so disagreement is moderate, no need for DBA/trimmed_mean with high trim. Adding these two improves RMSE while maintaining diversity.
changes: added trimmed_mean_r0.15 for low n_windows regularization
added topk_mean_per_horizon_k15 for better variance reduction with few windows
statistician.when_good: When relative_spread_mean < 0.25 and n_unique_winners >= 3, focus on adding selection candidates from universe leaderboards that beat current best RMSE.
